description: A garden classic, strawberries are one of the most rewarding fruits for beginners. 'Cambridge Favourite' is a mid-season variety known for its reliability, heavy crops, and excellent flavor. Perfect for pots, hanging baskets, or balcony troughs.

characteristics.waterNeeds: high and THEN info: High — keep well-watered when fruiting

pinchOutTops.info: Not typically required for this plant.

care.sowIndoors: Sow tiny seeds on the surface of fine compost in early spring. Keep at 18–21°C. Germination can be slow (2–4 weeks).
care.hardenOff: Gradually acclimatise young plants to outdoor conditions over 7–10 days in April.
care.plantOut: Plant in final containers, ensuring the crown is at soil level. Too deep and it rots; too shallow and it dries out.
care.watering: Water regularly at the base, especially as fruits develop. Avoid wetting the leaves to prevent rot.
care.feeding: Feed every 2 weeks with a high-potash liquid fertiliser (like tomato feed) once flowers appear.
care.harvest: Pick when fully red all over. Hull them just before eating to keep them fresh.
care.overwintering: Cut back old foliage in late summer. Clear away straw or mulch to prevent pests. The plant will return next spring.

propagation.seedDescription: Tiny, light brown achenes found on the surface of the fruit.
propagation.divisionMethod: In late summer, strawberries produce 'runners' (long stems with baby plants). Pin these into small pots of compost; once rooted, snip from the parent.

deadheading.notes: Remove runners during the fruiting season to focus the plant's energy on berries. Cut off dead leaves after the harvest.

seedSaving.method: Mash a very ripe strawberry and smear it on a paper towel. Let it dry, then scrape off the seeds.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Seeds are the tiny dots (achenes) on the outside of the red fruit.

funFact: Strawberries are the only fruit that wear their seeds on the outside—an average strawberry has about 200 seeds!
